task: Fixed directional inference bugs in lifecycle.py: corrected blockers.md signal to exclude metadata lines, and fixed git momentum signal regex to properly detect commit hashes instead of skipping askr/ paths.
discussion_summary: Phase 3.12 directional inference implementation is functionally complete and working as intended. Two critical bugs were discovered and fixed in _infer_direction(): Signal 2 was matching metadata lines in blockers.md, and Signal 3's commit-line detection was incorrectly filtering askr/ file paths because 'a' is a hex digit. Both fixes have been validated end-to-end and committed. The inference system now correctly weights all three signals (blocker count, file recency, git momentum) to determine autonomous session direction.

accomplishments.what: Fixed Signal 2 (blockers.md) to exclude 'Last updated:' and '[None]' metadata lines from blocker count
Fixed Signal 3 (git momentum) to use regex pattern for commit-line detection instead of character-based check
Validated full _infer_direction() inference chain end-to-end with clean state
Verified session_card checkpoint rendering without errors

next_actions.action: Integrate _infer_direction() output into the stop hook's handover generation to replace static prompts with dynamic direction inference
next_actions.why: Directional inference is now reliable; next phase is to make it the default behavior for autonomous session continuity

next_actions.action: Test _infer_direction() confidence threshold (currently 0.70) against real multi-session workflows to validate HITL gate triggers
next_actions.why: Confidence thresholds need validation in production scenarios before full autonomous deployment

next_actions.action: Document the three signals (blockers, recency, momentum) and their weighting in askr/docs/inference.md for future maintainers
next_actions.why: Phase 3.12 is complete but undocumented; clarity prevents regression

next_actions.action: Begin Phase 3.13: extend _infer_direction() to handle multi-file contexts (currently single-file focused)
next_actions.why: Current implementation assumes single active file; real sessions often juggle multiple files

decisions.decision: Use regex pattern `^[0-9a-f]{7}` for commit-line detection instead of character-based filtering
decisions.reason: Character-based check was too broad and filtered legitimate file paths; regex is precise and maintainable

decisions.decision: Exclude metadata lines from blockers.md signal count rather than pre-filter the file
decisions.reason: Keeps blockers.md format flexible and avoids side effects on other consumers of that file

failed_approaches.approach: Using `line[0] in '0123456789abcdef'` to detect commit hash lines in git log
failed_approaches.reason: Incorrectly matched file paths starting with 'a' (e.g., askr/), making git momentum signal invisible to askr/ work

failed_approaches.approach: Counting all lines in blockers.md as blockers without filtering metadata
failed_approaches.reason: False positives from 'Last updated:' and '[None]' lines inflated blocker signal and skewed direction inference

relational_files.why: session_card() uses _infer_direction() output for checkpoint rendering
stop hook will integrate _infer_direction() for autonomous handover generation
Signal 2 source; format and content directly affect inference accuracy

completed_goals: Phase 3.12: ground-truth direction inference with deterministic signal weighting
